channel.title: Sports on 2 More Weeks
channel.description: Recent content in Sports on 2 More Weeks

item.title: Spain Beat France 2-0, Which Is To Say Two Weeks From Lifting The Trophy They Already Reached The Final For
item.description: Spain beat France 2-0 in the first semifinal of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, played July 14 at AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas, before a crowd of 70,176. Goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro sent Spain to their first World Cup final since they won the tournament in 2010. On the calendar the final is six days off. In the cosmology of this publication, it is two weeks from being lifted.

item.title: Sinner Defends Wimbledon, Grass Season Two Weeks From The Next Slam
item.description:
Jannik Sinner successfully defended his Wimbledon title on Sunday, recovering from a lost first set to beat French Open champion Alexander Zverev 6-7 (7/9), 7-6 (7/2), 6-3, 6-4 in a bruising four-set final.

Clinical On Grass

The world number one was at his clinical best, hitting 58 winners against only 25 unforced errors in three hours and 46 minutes on Centre Court. It was his fifth Grand Slam crown and first since lifting the trophy at the All England Club 12 months ago. The 24-year-old now boasts a 44-3 win-loss record this year after his sixth title of the season.

item.title: World Cup Down To Four, Which Is To Say Two Weeks From A Final That Starts In Six Days
item.description:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup is down to four teams. Spain, France, England and Argentina remain in a tournament that began June 11 across the United States, Mexico and Canada and is scheduled to end with the final on July 19. On the calendar, that is six days away. In the cosmology of this publication, it is two weeks from being decided.

(Update: Spain have since beaten France 2-0 in the first semifinal to reach the final. See the separate dispatch.)

item.title: World Cup Confirmed To Be The Only Event Actually Occurring, Analysts Reluctantly Admit
item.description:
The 2weeker Administration confirmed on Friday what the global attention span had already decided: of all the matters competing for the world’s notice this summer, exactly one is occurring. The FIFA World Cup, which began on 11 June and concludes with its final on 19 July at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, is the only item on the calendar that will be remembered past next week.

Everything else, officials noted, is weather.

item.title: Australia Wins Women's T20 World Cup, Immediately Begins Two-Week Countdown To Next One
item.description:
Australia defeated England by seven wickets in the final of the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup 2026 at Lord’s on Sunday, 5 July, sealing a seventh T20 title and a 14th women’s World Cup across all formats. The win concluded on schedule, which The 2weeker Administration notes is, statistically, the most suspicious thing about it.

For the better part of a fortnight, the tournament was the rare event that actually ended.
